evals.expected_output: Creates project_requirements.md first with only engineering/setup items such as AI API, size, language, paths, output format, and build tools; after approval, creates experiment_outline.md with poster/project content only and no engineering details such as AI API, file paths, output folders, build tools, or script names. It records logo/image placement decisions but keeps institutional logos out of AI-generated diagrams. Before final production, it defaults to template 12 while still offering the 3 selected LaTeX preview templates when selection is needed.

evals.prompt: I need a portrait 36x48 inch English PPT academic poster from this methods outline and dataset. First confirm only engineering requirements: AI interface, size, language, output path, file structure, and PPT export. Put all poster content decisions in the second outline confirmation. Keep it visually modular in portrait orientation. Avoid large blank areas while preserving a clean academic layout.
evals.expected_output: Uses two gates with strict separation: engineering-only project_requirements.md first, then poster/project content only in experiment_outline.md. The final poster uses the approved outline only, keeps a balanced multi-module portrait layout, avoids one-row single-module and one-column single-module layouts, checks for page overflow, module overflow, text clipping, and bottom blank space, and reduces excessive detail text to preserve visual beauty before final export.

evals.prompt: 我的实验流程、理论公式和结果图都比较复杂。我会给你Image 2.0的API文档和KEY，请先确认API、尺寸、语言、文件路径等工程项，不要在第一步确认poster内容。第二步再确认完整outline。workflow流程图是必要部分，结果展示要多种图表形式。
evals.expected_output: Does not mix poster content into the first gate and does not include engineering details in experiment_outline.md. It confirms workflow, theory, results, required image roles, and diverse chart forms as project content; uses Python for exact data; uses routed AI prompt templates only for suitable diagrams; uses the bundled make_image_transparent.py script for transparent PNG cleanup; keeps poster text concise and avoids a visibly blank footer.
